html:
“A survivor can use the tent at Dusk or Night.

Survivor recovers 2 Health per second and 1 Sanity per second but consumes 0.33 Hunger per second. It also reduces 1 Wetness per second and increases temperature 1 degree per second up to 40 degrees.”

-

The Tent is a Survival structure that can be used to sleep during Dusk and Night, like an immobile Straw Roll or Fur Roll. It requires 6 Silk, 4 Twigs, 3 Rope to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

Tents can be used 15 times before disappearing. If the player destroys one with a Hammer, the number of resources returned is inversely proportional to number of times the player's used the Tent (the more times the Tent is used, the fewer resources it will drop when demolished).

Sleeping in a Tent affects stats over time rather than instantly. Using a Tent restores 1 and 2 per second but costs 1 per second. It also reduces Wetness by 1 per second, and raises Body Temperature by 1 per second, up to 40 degrees. All outside temperature sources are ignored, so the player's temperature will not increase or decrease if it is above 40. At least 26 Hunger is required before sleeping in a Tent.

The Tent can protect against the environment (e.g. Wetness, Freezing) while sleeping. The player is also immune to any damage except fire while using the tent.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Loyal World's Greatest Big Top Tent skin was awarded for watching 6 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from January 3, 2019 to January 17, 2019, and from March 7, 2019 to March 14, 2019. It is also available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei points.
  • The Proof of Purchase Webby Hideout skin is obtained by purchasing the Webber Youtooz.
  • The Woven - Distinguished Webwam skin was awarded for playing Don't Starve Together during Hallowed Nights 2025.
Woven - Elegant
Seaside Collection
Beach Tent
Take a nap in the shade after a long day at the beach.
Woven - Elegant
Frondly Tent
So cozy, you'll never want to leaf!
Loyal
Side Show Collection
World's Greatest Big Top Tent
Have the World's Greatest Dreams.
See ingame
Woven - Elegant
Ancient Collection
Archaic Tent
Step inside, and dream of long-forgotten things.
Loyal
Art Nouveau Collection
Nouveau Tent
Fall asleep surrounded by the beauty of the natural world.
Proof of Purchase
Webby Hideout
Naptimes are more fun when you have a cozy hideaway to crawl into.
Woven - Distinguished
Hallowed Nights Collection
Webwam
Enjoy a nap surrounded by luxurious organic silk.

Tips

  • The Tent is unusable by Wickerbottom due to her insomnia.
  • Unequipping any items that lose durability while worn (e.g. Winter Hat, Miner Hat) before sleeping will save their durability.
  • It is most efficient to sleep with low health and sanity and with high hunger to stay asleep longer.
  • You should have food prepared to eat when waking up so health isn't lost from starvation.
  • Even though Wormwood cannot heal from eating food, the Tent allows him to deal with this restriction by converting Hunger points to Health (and Sanity). Assuming there is an abundant supply of food, Wormwood can repeatedly replenish his health this way in order to farm valuable Living Logs.
  • Wearing a Slurper, Belt of Hunger, Hibearnation Vest or Funcap or playing as Wolfgang, Wes, or Warly doesn't change the Hunger cost of sleeping in a Tent.

 Trivia

  • The Tent's original use was to allow the player to change their character. Crafting the tent cost more resources (15 Silk, 6 Twigs, and 3 Ropes), its crafting description was "Change your skin", and the Tent would be destroyed after one use. This functionality was changed in the 2013 Progress! update.

Bugs

  • Sometimes after a 100% torch is equipped before sleeping, after the character sleeps the torch will still have a durability of 20%, and it will not lose durability. Unequipping and equipping the torch again will fix the bug.
  • Sleeping while taking damage from Freezing may cause the edges of the screen to be stuck red. This will persist until the game is reloaded or if the character starts taking freezing damage again and warms back up.

"tent"
“I get crazy when I don't sleep.”
–Wilson
“I got all the badges in Girl Scouts.”
–Willow
“It is time for a mighty nap.”
–Wolfgang
“Sleep is but a preview of death.”
–Wendy
“I CAN REBOOT IN THERE”
–WX-78
“Sleeping in there would give me a stiff neck.”
–Wickerbottom
“I'm used to sleeping in worse.”
–Woodie
“That's a bit rustic for my taste.”
–Maxwell
“Sleep this night, and prepare för battle ön the mörröw.”
–Wigfrid
“We should get some rest.”
–Webber
“For roughing it.”
–Warly
“Little home”
–Wormwood
“Putting the tent together is the best part of camping.”
–Winona
“I could catch a wink, I think.”
–Wortox
“Sleeping place.”
–Wurt
“I always sleep better in a tent.”
–Walter
“I guess it wouldn't hurt to take a quick little nap...”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Lều là một cấu trúc Sinh tồn có thể được sử dụng để ngủ trong Hoàng hôn và Đêm, giống như Cuộn Rơm hoặc Cuộn Lông bất động. Nó cần 6 Tơ, 4 Cành Cây, 3 Dây Thừng để chế tạo và Máy Luyện Kim để nguyên mẫu.

It also reduces 1 Wetness per second and increases temperature 1 degree per second up to 40 degrees.}} The '''Tent''' is a [[Survival]] [[structure]] that can be used to sleep during [[Day-Night Cycle|Dusk and Night]], like an immobile [[Straw Roll]] or [[Fur Roll]]. It requires 6 [[Silk]], 4 [[Twigs]], 3 [[Rope]] to craft and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. Tents can be used 15 times before disappearing. If the player destroys one with a [[Hammer]], the number of resources returned is inversely proportional to number of times the player's used the Tent (the more times the Tent is used, the fewer resources it will drop when demolished). Sleeping in a Tent affects stats over time rather than instantly. Using a Tent restores 1 {{Sanity}} and 2 {{Hp}} per second but costs 1 {{Hunger}} per second. It also reduces [[Wetness]] by 1 per second, and raises [[Freezing|Body Temperature]] by 1 per second, up to 40 degrees. All outside temperature sources are ignored, so the player's temperature will not increase or decrease if it is above 40. At least 26 [[Hunger]] is required before sleeping in a Tent. The Tent can protect against the environment (e.g. [[Wetness/DST|Wetness]], [[Freezing]]) while sleeping. The player is also immune to any damage except fire while using the tent. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Structure Skins|Belongings}} * The '''Loyal World's Greatest Big Top Tent''' skin was awarded for watching 6 hours of ''Don't Starve Together'' on Twitch from January 3, 2019 to January 17, 2019, and from March 7, 2019 to March 14, 2019. It is also available in the Klei Rewards shop for 1,200 Klei points. * The '''Proof of Purchase Webby Hideout''' skin is obtained by purchasing the Webber [[Youtooz]]. * The '''Woven - Distinguished Webwam''' skin was awarded for playing ''Don't Starve Together'' during Hallowed Nights 2025.

=={{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips== * The Tent is unusable by [[Wickerbottom]] due to her insomnia. * Unequipping any items that lose [[durability]] while worn (e.g. [[Winter Hat]], [[Miner Hat]]) before sleeping will save their durability. * It is most efficient to sleep with low health and sanity and with high hunger to stay asleep longer. * You should have food prepared to eat when waking up so health isn't lost from starvation. *Even though [[Wormwood]] cannot heal from eating food, the Tent allows him to deal with this restriction by converting Hunger points to Health (and Sanity). Assuming there is an abundant supply of food, Wormwood can repeatedly replenish his health this way in order to farm valuable [[Living Log]]s. * Wearing a [[Slurper]], [[Belt of Hunger]], [[Hibearnation Vest]] or [[Funcap]] or playing as [[Wolfgang]], [[Wes]], or [[Warly]] doesn't change the [[Hunger]] cost of sleeping in a Tent. =={{pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * The Tent's original use was to allow the player to change their character. Crafting the tent cost more resources (15 Silk, 6 Twigs, and 3 Ropes), its crafting description was "''Change your skin''", and the Tent would be destroyed after one use. This functionality was changed in the 2013 [[Don't Starve/Version History#January 29, 2013 – Progress!|''Progress!'' update]]. =={{pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs== * Sometimes after a 100% torch is equipped before sleeping, after the character sleeps the torch will still have a durability of 20%, and it will not lose durability. Unequipping and equipping the torch again will fix the bug. * Sleeping while taking damage from [[Freezing]] may cause the edges of the screen to be stuck red.
